label: Telegram
description: Receive and reply to messages in Telegram chats and groups using a bot created via @BotFather.

form.title: Connect Telegram
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to receive and send messages through a Telegram bot.

methods.label: Bot Token
methods.description: Paste the token from @BotFather. Takes 30 seconds to set up — no OAuth dance, no redirect URLs.
methods.how_to:
## Create a Telegram Bot and get a Token

### Steps

1. Open Telegram and search for **@BotFather**.
2. Send `/newbot` and follow the prompts to choose a name and username for your bot.
3. BotFather will reply with a **token** that looks like `123456789:ABC-defGhIJKlmNoPQRsTUVwxyZ`.
4. Copy the token and paste it into the **Bot Token** field below.

### Notes

- The bot must be started by each user before it can send them messages (the user sends `/start` to the bot once).
- For group chats, add the bot to the group and it will respond to messages where it is @mentioned.
- To get your bot's username, send `/mybots` to @BotFather.

fields.name: bot_token
fields.label: Bot Token
fields.description: Token from @BotFather. Starts with your bot's numeric ID followed by a colon.

fields.name: bot_username
fields.label: Bot Username (optional)
fields.description: Optional. The bot's @username without the @ sign. Used to detect @mentions in group chats so Anton only responds when addressed directly.
